verses.text: He eats the scroll at God's command, and 'it was in my mouth as honey for sweetness.' God's word, even of woe, is sweet. To receive His truth fully is to find it good, however hard its content.
He eats the scroll at God's command, and 'it was in my mouth as honey for sweetness.' God's word, even of woe, is sweet. To receive His truth fully is to find it good, however hard its content.
He eats the scroll at God's command, and 'it was in my mouth as honey for sweetness.' God's word, even of woe, is sweet. To receive His truth fully is to find it good, however hard its content.
He is sent not to a foreign tongue but to Israel, who will not hear him because 'they will not hearken unto me.' Their refusal is against God. To reject His messenger is to reject Him.
He is sent not to a foreign tongue but to Israel, who will not hear him because 'they will not hearken unto me.' Their refusal is against God. To reject His messenger is to reject Him.
He is sent not to a foreign tongue but to Israel, who will not hear him because 'they will not hearken unto me.' Their refusal is against God. To reject His messenger is to reject Him.
He is sent not to a foreign tongue but to Israel, who will not hear him because 'they will not hearken unto me.' Their refusal is against God. To reject His messenger is to reject Him.
God makes his face strong as adamant against theirs; he must go to the captives and speak whether they hear or forbear. Hardness meets hardness. God steels His servant for a stubborn people.
God makes his face strong as adamant against theirs; he must go to the captives and speak whether they hear or forbear. Hardness meets hardness. God steels His servant for a stubborn people.
God makes his face strong as adamant against theirs; he must go to the captives and speak whether they hear or forbear. Hardness meets hardness. God steels His servant for a stubborn people.
God makes his face strong as adamant against theirs; he must go to the captives and speak whether they hear or forbear. Hardness meets hardness. God steels His servant for a stubborn people.
The Spirit lifts him, and he goes 'in bitterness, in the heat of my spirit,' the LORD's hand strong upon him. The call is weighty. Even a true prophet may feel the burden of his message keenly.
The Spirit lifts him, and he goes 'in bitterness, in the heat of my spirit,' the LORD's hand strong upon him. The call is weighty. Even a true prophet may feel the burden of his message keenly.
The Spirit lifts him, and he goes 'in bitterness, in the heat of my spirit,' the LORD's hand strong upon him. The call is weighty. Even a true prophet may feel the burden of his message keenly.
He sits among the captives at Tel-abib 'astonished' seven days before the word comes again. Silence first. There are times to sit still and feel the weight before speaking.
He sits among the captives at Tel-abib 'astonished' seven days before the word comes again. Silence first. There are times to sit still and feel the weight before speaking.
'I have made thee a watchman'; if he fails to warn the wicked, their blood is on him, but warning delivers his own soul. Solemn responsibility. The duty to speak truth makes silence a kind of guilt.
'I have made thee a watchman'; if he fails to warn the wicked, their blood is on him, but warning delivers his own soul. Solemn responsibility. The duty to speak truth makes silence a kind of guilt.
'I have made thee a watchman'; if he fails to warn the wicked, their blood is on him, but warning delivers his own soul. Solemn responsibility. The duty to speak truth makes silence a kind of guilt.
Even if a righteous man turns to sin and is not warned, he dies, but the faithful watchman saves both the man and himself. Vigilance matters. Love warns, and warning is a means of rescue.
Even if a righteous man turns to sin and is not warned, he dies, but the faithful watchman saves both the man and himself. Vigilance matters. Love warns, and warning is a means of rescue.
The glory appears in the plain; the Spirit sets him on his feet and bids him shut himself in his house. Preparation for the sign-acts. God orders even the prophet's seclusion.
The glory appears in the plain; the Spirit sets him on his feet and bids him shut himself in his house. Preparation for the sign-acts. God orders even the prophet's seclusion.
The glory appears in the plain; the Spirit sets him on his feet and bids him shut himself in his house. Preparation for the sign-acts. God orders even the prophet's seclusion.

They shall bind him, and God will make his tongue cleave to his mouth, opening it only to declare, 'Thus saith the Lord GOD.' He speaks only at God's bidding. The true prophet's words are wholly God's, given and withheld at His will.
